name: ledger canonicalization: payload -> canonical bytes -> sha256, fixed values (no live hub, no timestamps)
what: The byte-exact rules of docs/protocol.md 'Canonicalization'. Every independent ledger verifier (Python, TS, anything) must reproduce these EXACT strings and hashes. The integral-float row is the known live divergence: Python json.dumps(2.0) -> '2.0', JS JSON.stringify(2.0) -> '2' - a verifier using ECMA-262 number formatting verifies TAMPERED where the hub says INTACT (agora-0118 roadmap, highest-severity drift).
hash_rule: sha256((prev_hash + '\n' + canonical).encode('utf-8')).hexdigest(); prev_hash = '' for every case here

cases.why: integral floats keep .0 (the live Python/JS divergence)
small magnitudes use zero-padded exponents, not decimal expansion
large magnitudes switch to exponent at 1e+16
non-ASCII escaped lowercase-hex; keys sorted at every level; no whitespace
